conditions: The nearest right piling is visible but lacks clear texture, and the mid/right and left pilings appear as very hazy silhouettes with no detail. This matches conditions where the 14 ft piling is only a faded silhouette, indicating about 15 ft visibility.

Rightmost 4 ft piling is clear with good texture. The 11 ft piling behind it is visible but quite hazy and low-contrast, and the 14+ ft structures are only faint silhouettes. This matches the guideline where the 14 ft piling is hazy/faded, indicating about 15 ft visibility.

Closest 4 ft piling at right is sharp with clear texture. The mid/right and back-left pilings (~11–14 ft and beyond) are only faint silhouettes with little detail, and the seafloor is not visible. This matches the criterion where the 14 ft piling is a hazy silhouette rather than sharp.

The sandy bottom is clearly visible and multiple rows of pilings including those around 30 ft away show distinct edges and some texture, comparable to the exceptional reference rather than the hazier 25 ft case.

All pilings including the far ~30 ft ones are visible with fairly good texture detail, and the sandy bottom is clearly seen across the frame. Conditions are very similar to the exceptional 35 ft reference, so visibility is at the top of the scale.
